name: OpenMetadata
what_it_does: OpenMetadata is a unified metadata platform for data discovery, observability, and governance. It provides a central repository for cataloging data assets across 84+ connectors, with built-in lineage tracking, data quality testing, and collaboration tools.
use_cases: Building a centralized data catalog for discovering tables, dashboards, pipelines, and topics
Tracking end-to-end column-level data lineage across a modern data stack
Running no-code data quality tests and monitoring freshness, volume, and schema drift
Enforcing data governance policies with ownership assignment, tagging, and auto-classification
not_for: Small teams without a significant multi-source data landscape
Real-time event streaming platforms (it catalogs, not processes data)
Teams needing a fully managed SaaS with zero self-hosting
best_when: An organization has data spread across many warehouses, databases, and BI tools and needs a single discovery and governance layer with lineage and quality monitoring.
avoid_when: The data estate is small and simple, or the team lacks capacity to operate a Docker/Kubernetes deployment.

auth.notes: Supports SSO, OAuth, and JWT-based authentication. Role-based access control built in.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open-source. Enterprise commercial offerings may exist; not detailed in README.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured error responses; quality is typical for Java-based platforms.
known_agent_gotchas: No native MCP server; agents must use REST API directly
Docker deployment has significant resource requirements

security_score_components.security_notes: Open metadata/data catalog platform. JWT + OAuth2. Data lineage and schema metadata may reveal architecture. RBAC for data asset access. Self-hosted for sensitive data environments.
